nfr_inventory.statement: The system is built for small personal-tool scale (tens of users, low concurrency). A single-server deployment with a synchronous request cycle is sufficient; no background job queues, horizontal scaling, or caching layer is required.
nfr_inventory.rationale: ADR-0001 (flat monolith) + ADR-0006 (single-instance PaaS) + INV6 (single-server synchronous mandate) are the direct architectural expression of A13; no additional mechanism needed or permitted.

nfr_inventory.statement: No latency or response-time target stated in aPRD.
nfr_inventory.rationale: aPRD contains no C*/A* specifying a latency SLA; category is aPRD-silent and genuinely not required at A13 personal-tool scale.

nfr_inventory.statement: No availability or uptime SLA stated in aPRD.
nfr_inventory.rationale: aPRD contains no C*/A* specifying an uptime target; category is aPRD-silent. PaaS best-effort uptime (ADR-0006) is adequate for personal-tool use.

nfr_inventory.statement: Authentication delegates to an external OAuth or social-login provider (e.g. Google or GitHub). The system stores only the provider token and user profile reference; it does not store hashed passwords or manage password resets internally.
nfr_inventory.rationale: ADR-0005 (Google OAuth 2.0 delegation) + INV1 (no stored credentials invariant) structurally satisfy A2; C2 (Identity & Auth) is the realizing component.

nfr_inventory.statement: No specific compliance requirements apply. Standard data practices are used; no mandated audit logs, right-to-erasure data model, or data-residency hosting constraint.
nfr_inventory.rationale: A9 explicitly waives all compliance requirements; INV7 encodes this as a cross-slice invariant — no audit logs, erasure capability, or compliance mechanism needed.

nfr_inventory.statement: No data-residency hosting constraint applies (waived by A9).
nfr_inventory.rationale: A9 explicitly waives data-residency hosting constraints; INV7 confirms no slice requires geographic data placement controls.

nfr_inventory.statement: The product must be a web application.
nfr_inventory.rationale: ADR-0001 (single-deployment monolith), ADR-0004 (MPA/SSR), and ADR-0006 (PaaS HTTPS endpoint) together satisfy C1; C6 (Web Ingress) is the realizing component at the ingress seam.

nfr_inventory.statement: The system should be live within approximately two months.
nfr_inventory.rationale: ADR-0001 (flat monolith, minimal scaffolding) and ADR-0006 (PaaS, zero server-config cost) were both chosen explicitly to satisfy C2's two-month constraint; timeline is a topology-wide property with no single owning component.
